info.title: Supplier Request Management Web Services
info.description: Web services to manage the Supplier Requests

get.summary: Search the Supplier Request Template using input search string

parameters.name: searchStr
parameters.description: Search Criteria: A minimum of 2 characters is required.

parameters.name: $top
parameters.description: Number of objects to return: default is 50, with a maximum limit of 1000.

parameters.name: $skip
parameters.description: Number of objects to skip when paginating through the search results: default is 0.

post.summary: Get a check-in ticket to upload files to FCS. This is required when check-in a file to a new document that has not yet been created.

parameters.name: $include
parameters.description: Specify the sub-resources to be returned by the web request. You can provide a combination of comma-separated entries, including the keywords 'none' and 'all', as well as payload-specific sub-resources such as [!]suppliers, [!]attachments, [!]detachAttachments, and [!]requestTemplate. Some entries are enabled by default, as described in the schema section of the response payload. For further details on refinement, please refer to the '6W REST Services Principles - Refinement' section.

parameters.name: $include
parameters.description: Specify the sub-resources to be returned by the web request. You can provide a combination of comma-separated entries, including the keywords 'none' and 'all', as well as payload-specific sub-resources such as [!]suppliers, [!]attachments, [!]requestTemplate, and [!]buyer. Some entries are enabled by default and are described in the schema section of the response payload. For more information about refinement, please refer to the '6W REST Services Principles - Refinement' section.

parameters.name: $include
parameters.description: Specify the sub-resources to be returned by the web request. You can provide a combination of comma-separated entries, including the keywords 'none' and 'all', along with payload-specific sub-resources such as [!]suppliers, [!]attachments, [!]detachAttachments, [!]requestTemplate, and [!]buyer. Some entries are enabled by default, as detailed in the schema section of the response payload. For more information on refinement, please refer to the '6W REST Services Principles - Refinement' section.

get.summary: Get attachments connected to Supplier Request
Search Supplier Requests using input search string

parameters.name: searchStr
parameters.description: Search Criteria: 2 or more characters required.

parameters.name: $top
parameters.description: Number of objects to return: default is 50, with a maximum limit of 1000.

parameters.name: $skip
parameters.description: Number of objects to skip when paginating through the search results: default is 0.

owner.description: The owner of the component request

receipt.description: check-in ticket generated using /servlet/fcs/checkin Public API for file Checkin

state.description: The state field to allow user to change the state of the object. Only applicable for Distribution Event object state modification

ticketURL.description: The URL to retrieve the file from the FCS server.

ticketparamname.description: The Ticket parameter name required by the FCS server.

ticket.description: The ticket parameter value required by the FCS server.

id.description: The attachment id which is to be detached

cestamp.description: The Concurrent Engineering (CESTAMP) of the object.

updateAction.description: Specify the sub-resource update action. Refer '6W Web Service Principles: Data Object Updates'.

identifier.description: The unique object identifier when an object is represented by a Public Resource URI.

source.description: The object server URL when an object is represented by a Public Resource URI.

relativePath.description: The object resource path when an object is represented by a Public Resource URI.

csrf.description: CSRF token is required to be sent back for update operations and may be provided with read operations.

success.description: The response data when the request succeeds with 200 status code.
Reflects true to indicate successful request

error-400.description: The response data when the request fails with 400 or 500 error.

error.description: An NLS error message.  In the case of a 400 error, the message is precise.  In the case of 500 error, the message is generic.

internalError.description: The technical reason of the failure - usually associated with a 500 error; this is not an NLS message and reflects the MQL or runtime exception.

SecurityContext.name: SecurityContext
SecurityContext.description: Security Context setting for the web request

ENO_CSRF_TOKEN.name: ENO_CSRF_TOKEN
ENO_CSRF_TOKEN.description: The ENOVIA CSRF token which is typically required for update operations.  Retrieve using {server}/resources/v1/application/CSRF

fields.name: $fields
fields.description: Specify the resource and sub-resource fields to be returned by the web request. Fields include the keywords 'none' and 'all', as well as payload-specific fields - see the schema response payload for details. For more information, refer to the '6W REST Services Principles - Refinement' section.
